tldr: Continue is an OSS AI coding assistant for IDEs; MindsDB is an open-source federated data engine that exposes AI as SQL over many data sources. Migration means leaving an IDE coding workflow for an SQL-native AI-on-data workflow.
why_switch: You want AI features over databases via SQL
Your problem is data-grounded prediction, not coding help
You prefer an OSS engine you can self-host
You want to expose agents as AI-Tables to apps
You no longer need IDE chat as your primary interface
what_to_export: Continue config file and workspace overrides
List of repos where Continue is configured
export_method: Continue keeps its config in plain files under your home directory (typically ~/.continue/config.json or config.ts) and optional workspace overrides. Copy these files for reference and note your model provider settings. Capture any custom slash commands or prompt files you wrote. Chat history is local; archive it if you need to keep it. Continue does not offer a structured export; treat this as a backup of dotfiles before uninstall. Nothing here maps directly into MindsDB, so this is a clean break rather than a data move.
import_method: MindsDB has no Continue importer. Deploy MindsDB self-hosted or use their cloud, connect data sources with CREATE DATABASE statements, and create AI-Tables with CREATE MODEL to ground LLMs in your data. Call those AI-Tables from your applications via standard SQL drivers. If your Continue workflow was about getting AI suggestions inside the IDE, MindsDB does not replace that; it lives in the data layer. Consider keeping Continue for personal coding workflows while using MindsDB for the data-grounded AI you actually need.
gotchas: These products solve different problems; verify scope before migrating.
MindsDB's license on GitHub is non-standard (NOASSERTION); review terms.
SQL-on-everything abstraction can mask performance issues.
You may still want Continue for personal IDE workflows.
Enterprise scale support may need a paid tier.
No data export from Continue is meaningful for MindsDB.

email.subject: Scope review: from Continue to MindsDB for AI on data
email.body:
Hi team,

Over the next 14 days we will pilot MindsDB for AI workflows over our databases. Continue stays available for IDE coding help; this is a re-platforming of our data-grounded AI work, not a swap.

Please flag any Continue features you rely on so we can plan accordingly. Configurations and access lists for the new MindsDB project will follow.

Thanks,
Your customer
